briefdescription.para: Free a string if it is not owned by the "dict" dictionary in the current scope.

memberdef.name: XML_NSMAP_POP
memberdef.initializer: i = (m)->last; \ (m)->last = (i)->prev; \ if ((m)->last == NULL) \ (m)->first = NULL; \ else \ (m)->last->next = NULL; \ (i)->next = (m)->pool; \ (m)->pool = i;

briefdescription.para: Create an empty entity reference node.

detaileddescription.para: This function doesn't attempt to look up the entity in doc.
name is consumed.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Fix up namespace declarations.

detaileddescription.para: This function tries to locate a namespace definition in a tree ancestors, or create a new namespace definition node similar to ns trying to reuse the same prefix. However if the given prefix is null (default namespace) or reused within the subtree defined by tree or on one of its ancestors then a new prefix is generated.

parameterdescription.para: a node expected to hold the new namespace

simplesect.para: the (new) namespace definition or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlNode *node, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *nsName, int useDTD)
memberdef.name: xmlGetPropNodeInternal

briefdescription.para: Unlink a node from its tree.

detaileddescription.para: This function only unlinks the node from the tree. It doesn't clear references to DTD nodes.

briefdescription.para: Do an entity lookup in the DTD entity hash table and.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the DTD to search

simplesect.para: the corresponding entity, if found.

briefdescription.para: Do an entity lookup in the DTD parameter entity hash table and.

parameterdescription.para: A pointer to the DTD to search

simplesect.para: the corresponding entity, if found.

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlChar *ncname, const xmlChar *prefix, xmlChar *memory, int len)
memberdef.name: xmlBuildQName

briefdescription.para: Build a QName from prefix and local name.

detaileddescription.para: Builds the QName prefix:ncname in memory if there is enough space and prefix is not NULL nor empty, otherwise allocate a new string. If prefix is NULL or empty it returns ncname.

simplesect.para: the new string which must be freed by the caller if different from memory and ncname or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML qualified name.

xrefdescription.para: This function doesn't report malloc failures.

para.verbatim:
[NS 5] QName ::= (Prefix ':')? LocalPart
[NS 6] Prefix ::= NCName
[NS 7] LocalPart ::= NCName

simplesect.para: NULL if the name doesn't have a prefix. Otherwise, returns the local part, and prefix is updated to get the Prefix. Both the return value and the prefix must be freed by the caller.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML qualified name.

simplesect.para: NULL if it is not a Qualified Name, otherwise, update len with the length in byte of the prefix and return a pointer to the start of the name without the prefix

briefdescription.para: Parse a QName.

detaileddescription.para: The return value points to the start of the local name in the input string. If the QName has a prefix, it will be allocated and stored in prefixPtr. This string must be freed by the caller. If there's no prefix, prefixPtr is set to NULL.

simplesect.para: the local name or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of NCName.

parameterdescription.para: allow spaces in front and end of the string

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of QName.

parameterdescription.para: allow spaces in front and end of the string

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of Name.

parameterdescription.para: allow spaces in front and end of the string

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Check that a value conforms to the lexical space of NMToken.

parameterdescription.para: allow spaces in front and end of the string

simplesect.para: 0 if this validates, a positive error code number otherwise and -1 in case of internal or API error.

briefdescription.para: Create a new namespace.

detaileddescription.para: For a default namespace, prefix should be NULL. The namespace URI in href is not checked. You should make sure to pass a valid URI.

If node is provided, it must be an element node. The namespace will be appended to the node's namespace declarations. It is an error if the node already has a definition for the prefix or default namespace.

simplesect.para: a new namespace pointer or NULL if arguments are invalid, the prefix is already in use or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Set the namespace of an element or attribute node.

detaileddescription.para: Passing a NULL namespace unsets the namespace.

briefdescription.para: Free an xmlNs object.
Free a list of xmlNs objects.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.name: xmlNewDtd

briefdescription.para: Create a DTD node.

detaileddescription.para: If a document is provided, it is an error if it already has an external subset. If the document has no external subset, it will be set to the created DTD.
To create an internal subset, use xmlCreateIntSubset.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new DTD object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Get the internal subset of a document.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the DTD object or NULL if not found.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDoc *doc, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *publicId, const xmlChar *systemId)
memberdef.name: xmlCreateIntSubset

briefdescription.para: Create a DTD node.

detaileddescription.para: If a document is provided and it already has an internal subset, the existing DTD object is returned without creating a new object. If the document has no internal subset, it will be set to the created DTD.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new or existing DTD object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Free a DTD structure.

parameterdescription.para: the DTD structure to free up

briefdescription.para: Creates a new XML document.

detaileddescription.para: If version is NULL, "1.0" is used.

simplesect.para: a new document or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Free a document including all children and associated DTDs.

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlDoc *doc, xmlAttr *attr, const xmlChar *value, size_t len, xmlNode **listPtr)
memberdef.name: xmlNodeParseAttValue

briefdescription.para: Parse an attribute value and replace the node's children with the resulting node list.

detaileddescription.para: content is expected to be a valid XML attribute value possibly containing character and entity references. Syntax errors and references to undeclared entities are ignored silently. Only references are handled, nested elements, comments or PIs are not.
This function is used by the SAX parser to parse attribute values a second time if entities aren't substituted. It's also used to parse node content in the tree API, but this is a historical mistake.

parameterdescription.para: maximum length of the attribute value
pointer to the resulting node list (optional)

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: See xmlStringGetNodeList.

parameterdescription.para: maximum length of the attribute value

simplesect.para: a pointer to the first child or NULL if the value if empty or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Parse an attribute value and build a node list with text and entity reference nodes.

detaileddescription.para: The resulting nodes will be associated with the document if provided. The document is also used to look up entities.
The input is not validated. Syntax errors or references to undeclared entities will be ignored silently with unspecified results.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the first child or NULL if the value if empty or a memory allocation failed.

parameterdescription.para: whether to escape characters and keep entity refs

simplesect.para: a pointer to the string.

briefdescription.para: Serializes attribute children (text and entity reference nodes) into a string.

detaileddescription.para: If inLine is true, entity references will be substituted. Otherwise, entity references will be kept and special characters like & as well as non-ASCII chars will be escaped. See xmlEncodeEntitiesReentrant for details. If list is the child of an attribute, escaping rules apply are adjusted.
See xmlNodeListGetRawString for an alternative option.

parameterdescription.para: a node list of attribute children

simplesect.para: a string or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Serializes attribute children (text and entity reference nodes) into a string.

detaileddescription.para: If inLine is true, entity references will be substituted. Otherwise, entity references will be kept and special characters like & will be escaped. See xmlEncodeSpecialChars for details.

parameterdescription.para: a node list of attribute children

simplesect.para: a string or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlNodePtr node, xmlNsPtr ns, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *value, int eatname)
memberdef.name: xmlNewPropInternal

briefdescription.para: Create an attribute node.

detaileddescription.para: If provided, value should be a raw, unescaped string.
If node is provided, the created attribute will be appended without checking for duplicate names. It is an error if node is not an element.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the attribute or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an attribute node.

detaileddescription.para: If provided, value should be a raw, unescaped string.
If node is provided, the created attribute will be appended without checking for duplicate names. It is an error if node is not an element.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the attribute

simplesect.para: a pointer to the attribute or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an attribute node.

detaileddescription.para: Like xmlNewNsProp, but the name string will be used directly without making a copy. Takes ownership of name which will also be freed on error.

parameterdescription.para: the local name of the attribute

simplesect.para: a pointer to the attribute or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an attribute node.

detaileddescription.para: If provided, value is expected to be a valid XML attribute value possibly containing character and entity references. Syntax errors and references to undeclared entities are ignored silently. If you want to pass a raw string, see xmlNewProp.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the attribute or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Free an attribute list including all children.

parameterdescription.para: the first attribute in the list

briefdescription.para: Free an attribute including all children.
Unlink and free an attribute including all children.

detaileddescription.para: Note this doesn't work for namespace declarations.
The attribute must have a non-NULL parent pointer.

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 if the attribute was not found or arguments are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Create a processing instruction object.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a processing instruction node.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlNewDocPI.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an element node.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlNewDocNode.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an element node.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlNewDocNodeEatName.
Like xmlNewNode, but the name string will be used directly without making a copy. Takes ownership of name which will also be freed on error.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNsPtr ns, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *content)
memberdef.name: xmlNewElem

briefdescription.para: Create an element node.

detaileddescription.para: If provided, content is expected to be a valid XML attribute value possibly containing character and entity references. Syntax errors and references to undeclared entities are ignored silently. Only references are handled, nested elements, comments or PIs are not. See xmlNewDocRawNode for an alternative.
General notes on object creation:

Each node and all its children are associated with the same document. The document should be provided when creating nodes to avoid a performance penalty when adding the node to a document tree. Note that a document only owns nodes reachable from the root node. Unlinked subtrees must be freed manually.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an element node.

detaileddescription.para: Like xmlNewDocNode, but the name string will be used directly without making a copy. Takes ownership of name which will also be freed on error.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an element node.

detaileddescription.para: If provided, value should be a raw, unescaped string.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a document fragment node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a text node.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlNewDocText.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a new child element and append it to a parent element.

detaileddescription.para: If ns is NULL, the newly created element inherits the namespace of the parent.
If content is provided, a text node will be added to the child element, see xmlNewDocRawNode.

parameterdescription.para: raw text content of the child (optional)

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create an empty entity reference node.

detaileddescription.para: This function is MISNAMED. It doesn't create a character reference but an entity reference.
This function doesn't attempt to look up the entity in doc.
Entity names like &entity; are handled as well.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a new entity reference node, linking the result with the entity in doc if found.

detaileddescription.para: Entity names like &entity; are handled as well.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a new text node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a new text node.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlNewDocTextLen.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a new text node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlNewDocComment.

detaileddescription.para: Create a comment node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a CDATA section node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a comment node.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Associate all nodes in a tree with a new document.

detaileddescription.para: This is an internal function which shouldn't be used. It is invoked by functions like xmlAddChild, xmlAddSibling or xmlReplaceNode. tree must be the root node of an unlinked subtree.
Also copy strings from the old document's dictionary and remove ID attributes from the old ID table.

simplesect.para: 0 on success. If a memory allocation fails, returns -1. The whole tree will be updated on failure but some strings may be lost.

briefdescription.para: Associate all subtrees in list with a new document.

detaileddescription.para: Internal function, see xmlSetTreeDoc.

simplesect.para: 0 on success. If a memory allocation fails, returns -1. All subtrees will be updated on failure but some strings may be lost.

briefdescription.para: Create a new child element and append it to a parent element.

detaileddescription.para: If ns is NULL, the newly created element inherits the namespace of the parent.

If provided, content is expected to be a valid XML attribute value possibly containing character and entity references. Text and entity reference node will be added to the child element, see xmlNewDocNode.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the new node object or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr cur, xmlNodePtr parent, xmlNodePtr prev, xmlNodePtr next)
memberdef.name: xmlInsertProp

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr cur, xmlNodePtr parent, xmlNodePtr prev, xmlNodePtr next, int coalesce)
memberdef.name: xmlInsertNode

briefdescription.para: Unlinks cur and inserts it as next sibling after prev.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike xmlAddChild this function does not merge text nodes.
If cur is an attribute node, it is inserted after attribute prev. If the attribute list contains an attribute with a name matching cur, the old attribute is destroyed.
See the notes in xmlAddChild.

simplesect.para: cur or a sibling if cur was merged. Returns NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Unlinks cur and inserts it as previous sibling before next.

detaileddescription.para: Unlike xmlAddChild this function does not merge text nodes.
If cur is an attribute node, it is inserted before attribute next. If the attribute list contains an attribute with a name matching cur, the old attribute is destroyed.
See the notes in xmlAddChild.

simplesect.para: cur or a sibling if cur was merged. Returns NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Unlinks cur and inserts it as last sibling of node.

detaileddescription.para: If cur is a text node, it may be merged with an adjacent text node and freed. In this case the text node containing the merged content is returned.
If cur is an attribute node, it is appended to the attribute list containing node. If the attribute list contains an attribute with a name matching cur, the old attribute is destroyed.
See the notes in xmlAddChild.

simplesect.para: cur or a sibling if cur was merged. Returns NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Append a node list to another node.

detaileddescription.para: See xmlAddChild.

parameterdescription.para: the first node in the list

simplesect.para: the last child or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Unlink cur and append it to the children of parent.

detaileddescription.para: If cur is a text node, it may be merged with an adjacent text node and freed. In this case the text node containing the merged content is returned.
If cur is an attribute node, it is appended to the attributes of parent. If the attribute list contains an attribute with a name matching cur, the old attribute is destroyed.
Before version 2.13, this function didn't unlink cur before moving it. Callers must unlink the node manually if it has siblings.
General notes:

Move operations like xmlAddChild can cause element or attribute nodes to reference namespaces that aren't declared in one of their ancestors. This can lead to use-after-free errors if the elements containing the declarations are freed later, especially when moving nodes from one document to another. You should consider calling xmlReconciliateNs after a move operation to normalize namespaces. Another option is to call xmlDOMWrapAdoptNode with the target parent before moving a node.

For the most part, move operations don't check whether the resulting tree structure is valid. Users must make sure that parent nodes only receive children of valid types. Inserted child nodes must never be an ancestor of the parent node to avoid cycles in the tree structure. In general, only document, document fragments, elements and attributes should be used as parent nodes.
When moving a node between documents and a memory allocation fails, the node's content will be corrupted and it will be unlinked. In this case, the node must be freed manually.
Moving DTDs between documents isn't supported.

simplesect.para: cur or a sibling if cur was merged. Returns NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Find the last child of a node.

simplesect.para: the last child or NULL if parent has no children.

briefdescription.para: Count the number of child nodes which are elements.

detaileddescription.para: Note that entity references are not expanded.

simplesect.para: the number of element children or 0 if arguments are invalid.

briefdescription.para: Find the first child node which is an element.

detaileddescription.para: Note that entity references are not expanded.

simplesect.para: the first element or NULL if parent has no children.

briefdescription.para: Find the last child node which is an element.

detaileddescription.para: Note that entity references are not expanded.

simplesect.para: the last element or NULL if parent has no children.

briefdescription.para: Find the closest preceding sibling which is a element.

detaileddescription.para: Note that entity references are not expanded.

simplesect.para: the sibling or NULL if no sibling was found.

briefdescription.para: Find the closest following sibling which is a element.

detaileddescription.para: Note that entity references are not expanded.

simplesect.para: the sibling or NULL if no sibling was found.

briefdescription.para: Free a node list including all children.

parameterdescription.para: the first node in the list

briefdescription.para: Free a node including all the children.

detaileddescription.para: This doesn't unlink the node from the tree. Call xmlUnlinkNode first unless cur is a root node.

briefdescription.para: Unlink a node from its tree.

detaileddescription.para: The node is not freed. Unless it is reinserted, it must be managed manually and freed eventually by calling xmlFreeNode.

briefdescription.para: Unlink the old node.

detaileddescription.para: If cur is provided, it is unlinked and inserted in place of old.
It is an error if old has no parent.
Unlike xmlAddChild, this function doesn't merge text nodes or delete duplicate attributes.
See the notes in xmlAddChild.

simplesect.para: old or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a namespace.

simplesect.para: the copied namespace or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a namespace list.

simplesect.para: the head of the copied list or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a copy of the attribute.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the parent pointer of the copy to target but doesn't set the attribute on the target element. Users should consider to set the attribute by calling xmlAddChild afterwards or reset the parent pointer to NULL.

parameterdescription.para: the element where the attribute will be grafted

simplesect.para: the copied attribute or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Create a copy of an attribute list.

detaileddescription.para: This function sets the parent pointers of the copied attributes to target but doesn't set the attributes on the target element.

parameterdescription.para: the element where the attributes will be grafted

simplesect.para: the head of the copied list or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a node.

simplesect.para: the copy or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a node list.

detaileddescription.para: If parent is provided, sets the parent pointer of the copied nodes, but doesn't update the children and last pointer of parent.

simplesect.para: a the copy or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Copy a node.

detaileddescription.para: If extended is 0, make a shallow copy.
If extended is 1, make a deep copy (properties, namespaces and children when applicable).
If extended is 2, make a shallow copy including properties and namespaces of elements.
Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlDocCopyNode.

simplesect.para: the copied node or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a node into another document.

detaileddescription.para: If extended is 0, make a shallow copy.
If extended is 1, make a deep copy (properties, namespaces and children when applicable).
If extended is 2, make a shallow copy including properties and namespaces of elements.

simplesect.para: the copied node or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a node list and all children into a new document.

parameterdescription.para: the first node in the list.

simplesect.para: the head of the copied list or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a node list and all children.

detaileddescription.para: Use of this function is DISCOURAGED in favor of xmlDocCopyNodeList.

parameterdescription.para: the first node in the list.

simplesect.para: the head of the copied list or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a DTD.

simplesect.para: the copied DTD or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Copy a document.

detaileddescription.para: If recursive, the content tree will be copied too as well as DTD, namespaces and entities.

parameterdescription.para: if not zero do a recursive copy.

simplesect.para: the copied document or NULL if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Get line number of node.

detaileddescription.para: Try to work around the limitation of line numbers being stored as 16 bits ints. Requires xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_BIG_LINES to be set when parsing.

parameterdescription.para: used to limit any risk of recursion

simplesect.para: the line number if successful, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get line number of node.

detaileddescription.para: Try to work around the limitation of line numbers being stored as 16 bits ints. Requires xmlParserOption XML_PARSE_BIG_LINES to be set when parsing.

simplesect.para: the line number if successful, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Build a structure based Path for the given node.

simplesect.para: the new path or NULL in case of error. The caller must free the returned string

briefdescription.para: Get the root element of the document.

detaileddescription.para: Searches the document's children for the root element. The first child is not necessarily the root element, but could also be a DTD, comment or PI.

simplesect.para: the root element or NULL if no element was found.

briefdescription.para: If root is NULL no action is taken.

detaileddescription.para: To remove a node from a document, use xmlUnlinkNode instead.
Set the root element of the document (doc->children is a list containing possibly comments, PIs, etc ...).
root must be an element node. It is unlinked before insertion.

simplesect.para: the unlinked old root element or NULL if the document didn't have a root element or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Set the xml:lang attribute of a node.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 if arguments are invalid, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Find the xml:lang of a node.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the value of the xml:lang attribute or the one carried by the nearest ancestor.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the lang value, or NULL if not found It's up to the caller to free the memory with xmlFree.

briefdescription.para: Set the xml:space attribute of a node.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 if arguments are invalid, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Find the xml:space of a node.

detaileddescription.para: Look up the value of the xml:space attribute or the one carried by the nearest ancestor.

simplesect.para: -1 if xml:space is not inherited, 0 if "default", 1 if "preserve"

briefdescription.para: Set (or reset) the name of a node.
Set (or reset) the base URI of a node, i.e.

detaileddescription.para: the value of the xml:base attribute.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 on error.

briefdescription.para: Searches for the base URI.

detaileddescription.para: The code should work on both XML and HTML document even if base mechanisms are completely different. It returns the base as defined in RFC 2396 sections "5.1.1. Base URI within Document Content" and "5.1.2. Base URI from the Encapsulating Entity". However it does not return the document base (5.1.3), use doc->URL in this case.

parameterdescription.para: the document the node pertains to

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, 1 if a URI or argument is invalid, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: See xmlNodeGetBaseSafe.

detaileddescription.para: This function doesn't allow to distinguish memory allocation failures from a non-existing base.

parameterdescription.para: the document the node pertains to

simplesect.para: a pointer to the base URL, or NULL if not found It's up to the caller to free the memory with xmlFree.

briefdescription.para: Append the string value of a node to buffer.

detaileddescription.para: For text nodes, the string value is the text content. Otherwise, the string value is the concatenation of the string values of the node's descendants.
Entity references are substituted.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Append the string value of a node to buf.

detaileddescription.para: For text nodes, the string value is the text content. Otherwise, the string value is the concatenation of the string values of the node's descendants.
Entity references are substituted.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Returns the string value of a node.

detaileddescription.para: For text nodes, the string value is the text content. Otherwise, the string value is the concatenation of the string values of the node's descendants.
Entity references are substituted.
It's up to the caller to free the result with xmlFree.

simplesect.para: a new string or NULL if arguments are invalid or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Replace the text content of a node.

detaileddescription.para: Sets the raw text content of text, CDATA, comment or PI nodes.

For element and attribute nodes, removes all children and replaces them by parsing content which is expected to be a valid XML attribute value possibly containing character and entity references. Syntax errors and references to undeclared entities are ignored silently. Unfortunately, there isn't an API to pass raw content directly. An inefficient work-around is to escape the content with xmlEncodeSpecialChars before passing it. A better trick is clearing the old content with xmlNodeSetContent(node, NULL) first and then calling xmlNodeAddContent(node, content). Unlike this function, xmlNodeAddContent accepts raw text.

parameterdescription.para: the new value of the content

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 on error, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: See xmlNodeSetContent.

parameterdescription.para: the new value of the content

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 on error, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Append the extra substring to the node content.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: In contrast to xmlNodeSetContentLen, content is supposed to be raw text, so unescaped XML special chars are allowed, entity references are not supported.
This doesn't work on attributes before version 2.15.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 on error, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Append the extra substring to the node content.

detaileddescription.para: NOTE: In contrast to xmlNodeSetContent, content is supposed to be raw text, so unescaped XML special chars are allowed, entity references are not supported.
This doesn't work on attributes before version 2.15.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 on error, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Merge the second text node into the first.

detaileddescription.para: If first is NULL, second is returned. Otherwise, the second node is unlinked and freed.

parameterdescription.para: the second text node being merged

simplesect.para: the first text node augmented or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Find all in-scope namespaces of a node.

detaileddescription.para: out returns a NULL terminated array of namespace pointers that must be freed by the caller.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 if no namespaces were found, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Find all in-scope namespaces of a node.

detaileddescription.para: Use xmlGetNsListSafe for better error reporting.

simplesect.para: a NULL terminated array of namespace pointers that must be freed by the caller or NULL if no namespaces were found or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Search for a namespace with prefix in scope of node.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed, 1 on other errors.

briefdescription.para: Search for a namespace with prefix in scope of node.

detaileddescription.para: Recurse on the parents until it finds the defined namespace or return NULL otherwise.
If nameSpace is NULL, the default namespace is looked up.
Namespace search doesn't cross entity boundaries.

simplesect.para: the namespace pointer or NULL if no namespace was found or a memory allocation failed. Allocations can only fail if the xml namespace is queried.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr node, xmlNodePtr ancestor, const xmlChar *prefix)
memberdef.name: xmlNsInScope

briefdescription.para: Verify that the given namespace held on ancestor is still in scope on node.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if false and -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Search for a namespace matching URI in scope of node.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 if a memory allocation failed, 1 on other errors.

briefdescription.para: Search for a namespace matching URI in scope of node.

simplesect.para: the namespace pointer or NULL if no namespace was found or a memory allocation failed. Allocations can only fail if the xml namespace is queried.

briefdescription.para: This function checks that all the namespaces declared within the given tree are properly declared.

detaileddescription.para: This is needed for example after copy or cut and then paste operations. The subtree may still hold pointers to namespace declarations outside the subtree or invalid/masked. As much as possible the function tries to reuse the existing namespaces found in the new environment. If not possible the new namespaces are redeclared on tree at the top of the given subtree.

parameterdescription.para: a node defining the subtree to reconciliate

simplesect.para: 0 on success or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Search for an attribute of an element.

detaileddescription.para: This function also looks in DTD attribute declaration for #FIXED or default declaration values.

simplesect.para: the attribute or the attribute declaration or NULL if neither was found. Also returns NULL if a memory allocation failed, making this function unreliable.

briefdescription.para: Search for an attribute of an element.

detaileddescription.para: The attribute has to match the specified namespace. A namespace of NULL means that the attribute must have no namespace.
This function also looks in DTD attribute declaration for #FIXED or default declaration values.

simplesect.para: the attribute or the attribute declaration or NULL if neither was found. Also returns NULL if a memory allocation failed making this function unreliable.

memberdef.argsstring: (const xmlNode *node, const xmlChar *name, const xmlChar *nsUri, xmlChar **out)
memberdef.name: xmlNodeGetAttrValue

briefdescription.para: Look up the value of an element's attribute.

detaileddescription.para: The attribute has to match the specified namespace. A namespace of NULL means that the attribute must have no namespace.
Entities are substituted. The returned value must be freed by the caller.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 if no attribute was found, -1 if a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Look up the value of an element's attribute.

detaileddescription.para: Entities are substituted. The returned value must be freed by the caller.
This function looks in DTD attribute declarations for #FIXED or default declaration values.
NOTE: This function is ignores namespaces. Use xmlGetNsProp or xmlGetNoNsProp for namespace aware processing.
NOTE: This function doesn't allow to distinguish malloc failures from missing attributes.

simplesect.para: the attribute value or NULL if not found or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Look up the value of an element's attribute.

detaileddescription.para: Entities are substituted. The returned value must be freed by the caller.
This function looks in DTD attribute declarations for #FIXED or default declaration values.
This function is similar to xmlGetProp except it will accept only an attribute in no namespace.
NOTE: This function doesn't allow to distinguish malloc failures from missing attributes. It's more robust to use xmlNodeGetAttrValue.

simplesect.para: the attribute value or NULL if not found or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Look up the value of an element's attribute.

detaileddescription.para: The attribute has to match the specified namespace. A namespace of NULL means that the attribute must have no namespace.
Entities are substituted. The returned value must be freed by the caller.
This function looks in DTD attribute declaration for #FIXED or default declaration values.
NOTE: This function doesn't allow to distinguish malloc failures from missing attributes. It's more robust to use xmlNodeGetAttrValue.

simplesect.para: the attribute value or NULL if not found or a memory allocation failed.

briefdescription.para: Remove an attribute of an element.

detaileddescription.para: This handles only attributes in no namespace.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, -1 if not found

briefdescription.para: Remove an attribute of an element.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, -1 if not found

briefdescription.para: Set (or reset) an element's attribute.

detaileddescription.para: If name has a prefix, the corresponding namespace will be used. It is an error if there's no such binding for the prefix in scope.

simplesect.para: the attribute pointer.

briefdescription.para: Set (or reset) an element's attribute.

detaileddescription.para: The namespace must be in scope.

simplesect.para: the attribute pointer.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the node is a text node.

simplesect.para: 1 if yes, 0 if no

briefdescription.para: Checks whether this node is an empty or whitespace-only text node.

simplesect.para: 1 if yes, 0 if no

briefdescription.para: Concat the given string at the end of the existing node content.

detaileddescription.para: If len is -1, the string length will be calculated.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Get the compression level of a document, ZLIB based.

simplesect.para: 0 (uncompressed) to 9 (max compression)

briefdescription.para: Set the compression level of a document, ZLIB based.

detaileddescription.para: Correct values: 0 (uncompressed) to 9 (max compression)

briefdescription.para: Get the global compression level, ZLIB based.

simplesect.para: 0 (uncompressed) to 9 (max compression)

briefdescription.para: Set the global compression level, ZLIB based.

detaileddescription.para: Correct values: 0 (uncompressed) to 9 (max compression)

briefdescription.para: Frees the ns-map.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlNsMapPtr *nsmap, int position, xmlNsPtr oldNs, xmlNsPtr newNs, int depth)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapNsMapAddItem

briefdescription.para: Adds an ns-mapping item.

simplesect.para: the added item.

briefdescription.para: Creates or reuses an xmlNs struct on doc->oldNs with the given prefix and namespace name.

simplesect.para: the acquired ns struct or NULL in case of an API or internal error.

briefdescription.para: Allocates and initializes a new DOM-wrapper context.

simplesect.para: the xmlDOMWrapCtxt or NULL in case of an internal error.

briefdescription.para: Frees the DOM-wrapper context.
Searches for a ns-decl with the given prefix in nsList.

simplesect.para: the ns-decl if found, NULL if not found and on API errors.

briefdescription.para: Puts in-scope namespaces into the ns-map.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, -1 on API or internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlNsPtr **list, int *size, int *number, xmlNsPtr oldNs, xmlNsPtr newNs)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapNSNormAddNsMapItem2

briefdescription.para: Unlinks the given node from its owner.

detaileddescription.para: This will substitute ns-references to node->nsDef for ns-references to doc->oldNs, thus ensuring the removed branch to be autark wrt ns-references.
NOTE: This function was not intensively tested.

parameterdescription.para: set of options, unused at the moment

simplesect.para: 0 on success, 1 if the node is not supported, -1 on API and internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr node, const xmlChar *nsName, xmlNsPtr *retNs, int prefixed)
memberdef.name: xmlSearchNsByNamespaceStrict

briefdescription.para: Dynamically searches for a ns-declaration which matches the given nsName in the ancestor-or-self axis of node.

parameterdescription.para: if the found ns-decl must have a prefix (for attributes)

simplesect.para: 1 if a ns-decl was found, 0 if not and -1 on API and internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr node, const xmlChar *prefix, xmlNsPtr *retNs)
memberdef.name: xmlSearchNsByPrefixStrict

briefdescription.para: Dynamically searches for a ns-declaration which matches the given nsName in the ancestor-or-self axis of node.

simplesect.para: 1 if a ns-decl was found, 0 if not and -1 on API and internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr elem, const xmlChar *nsName, const xmlChar *prefix, int checkShadow)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapNSNormDeclareNsForced

briefdescription.para: Declares a new namespace on elem.

detaileddescription.para: It tries to use the given prefix. If a ns-decl with the given prefix is already existent on elem, it will generate an other prefix.

parameterdescription.para: the preferred prefix of the ns-decl
ensure that the new ns-decl doesn't shadow ancestor ns-decls

simplesect.para: 1 if a ns-decl was found, 0 if not and -1 on API and internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDocPtr doc, xmlNodePtr elem, xmlNsPtr ns, xmlNsPtr *retNs, xmlNsMapPtr *nsMap, int depth, int ancestorsOnly, int prefixed)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapNSNormAcquireNormalizedNs

briefdescription.para: Searches for a matching ns-name in the ns-decls of nsMap, if not found it will either declare it on elem, or store it in doc->oldNs.

detaileddescription.para: If a new ns-decl needs to be declared on elem, it tries to use the ns->prefix for it, if this prefix is already in use on elem, it will change the prefix or the new ns-decl.

parameterdescription.para: the element-node to declare namespaces on
the ns-struct to use for the search
if the searched ns-decl must have a prefix (for attributes)

simplesect.para: 0 if succeeded, -1 otherwise and on API/internal errors.

briefdescription.para: Fix up namespaces.

detaileddescription.para: Ensures that ns-references point to ns-decls hold on element-nodes. Ensures that the tree is namespace wellformed by creating additional ns-decls where needed. Note that, since prefixes of already existent ns-decls can be shadowed by this process, it could break QNames in attribute values or element content.
NOTE: This function was not intensively tested.

parameterdescription.para: DOM wrapper context, unused at the moment

simplesect.para: 0 if succeeded, -1 otherwise and on API/internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDOMWrapCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlDocPtr sourceDoc, xmlNodePtr node, xmlDocPtr destDoc, xmlNodePtr destParent, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapAdoptBranch

briefdescription.para: Ensures that ns-references point to destDoc: either to elements->nsDef entries if destParent is given, or to destDoc->oldNs otherwise.

detaileddescription.para: If destParent is given, it ensures that the tree is namespace wellformed by creating additional ns-decls where needed. Note that, since prefixes of already existent ns-decls can be shadowed by this process, it could break QNames in attribute values or element content.
NOTE: This function was not intensively tested.

parameterdescription.para: the optional context for custom processing
the optional new parent of node in destDoc

simplesect.para: 0 if succeeded, -1 otherwise and on API/internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDOMWrapCtxt *ctxt, xmlDoc *sourceDoc, xmlNode *node, xmlNode **resNode, xmlDoc *destDoc, xmlNode *destParent, int deep, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapCloneNode

briefdescription.para: Clone a node and fix namespaces.

detaileddescription.para: References of out-of scope ns-decls are remapped to point to destDoc. If destParent is given, then nsDef entries on element-nodes are used. If no destParent is given, then destDoc->oldNs entries are used. This is the case when you don't know already where the cloned branch will be added to.

If destParent is given, it ensures that the tree is namespace wellformed by creating additional ns-decls where needed. Note that, since prefixes of already existent ns-decls can be shadowed by this process, it could break QNames in attribute values or element content.

parameterdescription.para: the optional context for custom processing
the clone of the given node
the optional new parent of node in destDoc

simplesect.para: 0 if the operation succeeded, 1 if a node of unsupported (or not yet supported) type was given, -1 on API/internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDOMWrapCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlDocPtr sourceDoc, xmlAttrPtr attr, xmlDocPtr destDoc, xmlNodePtr destParent, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapAdoptAttr

briefdescription.para: attr is adopted by destDoc.

detaileddescription.para: Ensures that ns-references point to destDoc: either to elements->nsDef entries if destParent is given, or to destDoc->oldNs otherwise.

parameterdescription.para: the optional context for custom processing
the optional new parent of attr in destDoc

simplesect.para: 0 if succeeded, -1 otherwise and on API/internal errors.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlDOMWrapCtxt *ctxt, xmlDoc *sourceDoc, xmlNode *node, xmlDoc *destDoc, xmlNode *destParent, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlDOMWrapAdoptNode

briefdescription.para: Fix up namespaces before moving a node.

detaileddescription.para: References of out-of scope ns-decls are remapped to point to destDoc: If destParent is given, then nsDef entries on element-nodes are used. If no destParent is given, then destDoc->oldNs entries are used This is the case when you have an unlinked node and just want to move it to the context of.

If destParent is given, it ensures that the tree is namespace wellformed by creating additional ns-decls where needed. Note that, since prefixes of already existent ns-decls can be shadowed by this process, it could break QNames in attribute values or element content.
NOTE: This function was not intensively tested.

parameterdescription.para: the optional context for custom processing
the optional new parent of node in destDoc

simplesect.para: 0 if the operation succeeded, 1 if a node of unsupported type was given, 2 if a node of not yet supported type was given and -1 on API/internal errors.

briefdescription.para: Try to find if the document correspond to an XHTML DTD.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if not and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Registers a callback for node creation.

parameterdescription.para: function pointer to the new RegisterNodeFunc

simplesect.para: the old value of the registration function

briefdescription.para: Registers a callback for node destruction.

parameterdescription.para: function pointer to the new DeregisterNodeFunc

simplesect.para: the previous value of the deregistration function
